title: Outsmart Online Scams in 2025

modules.title: Module 1: How AI Has Supercharged Scam Operations
modules.sub: Understanding the technology turning scammers into scaled threats
modules.description: Learners explore how generative AI, voice cloning, and automation have transformed scamming from a cottage industry into a high-volume operation. They examine real-world case studies that show the scale, speed, and sophistication modern tools give bad actors. By the end, learners can articulate why yesterday's scam-spotting instincts are no longer enough.

modules.title: Module 2: The Anatomy of a Modern AI-Generated Scam
modules.sub: Dissecting the structure, psychology, and mechanics behind each attack
modules.description: Learners break down a scam message layer by layer — from the hook and emotional trigger to the call to action and cover story. They study how AI personalizes content using harvested data to make attacks feel legitimate and urgent. This analysis gives learners a mental model they can apply to any suspicious communication they encounter.

modules.title: Module 3: Spotting Red Flags Across Email, SMS, and Social Media
modules.sub: Recognizing channel-specific warning signs before you click or respond
modules.description: Learners practice identifying red flags tailored to each communication channel, including spoofed sender addresses, smishing links, and deepfake-driven social media personas. They work through realistic examples and apply a quick-check framework to decide whether a message is safe, suspicious, or a confirmed threat. The module builds the pattern-recognition muscle needed to catch scams that look almost perfect.

modules.title: Module 4: What to Do When You Suspect or Fall for a Scam
modules.sub: Executing a calm, confident response plan when seconds matter
modules.description: Learners follow a step-by-step response playbook covering actions to take immediately after suspecting or confirming they have been targeted — from freezing accounts to contacting their bank. They also work through the harder scenario of having already engaged, mapping out damage-limitation moves and documentation steps. The goal is to replace panic with a practiced, protective routine.

modules.title: Module 5: Protecting Your Household — Kids, Parents, and Everyone Between
modules.sub: Tailoring scam awareness for every age and vulnerability level at home
modules.description: Learners examine how scammers specifically target children, older adults, and other vulnerable household members with age-appropriate lures and manipulative tactics. They create a simple household safety plan that includes shared rules, safe communication habits, and designated check-in contacts. The module empowers learners to act as a first line of defense for the people they live with.

modules.title: Module 6: Reporting Scams and Supporting Others Who Have Been Targeted
modules.sub: Turning your experience into protection for your wider community
modules.description: Learners map out the key reporting channels — from the FTC and FBI's IC3 to platform-level abuse tools — and practice submitting an accurate, complete report. They also learn how to offer non-judgmental support to someone who has been scammed, recognizing the shame and distress that often prevents victims from seeking help. By reporting and supporting, learners extend their protective impact beyond themselves.

modules.title: Module 7: Building Personal Habits That Make You a Hard Target
modules.sub: Locking in lasting behaviors that reduce your risk every day
modules.description: Learners audit their current digital habits against a set of high-impact protective behaviors, including multi-factor authentication, data minimization, and regular privacy checkups. They build a personalized action plan that prioritizes the two or three changes most likely to reduce their specific risk profile. The module closes the course by shifting the learner's identity from passive potential victim to active, resilient defender.

synopsis: Every year, AI-powered scams drain billions from ordinary people who had no idea the message they trusted was machine-crafted and psychologically tuned to exploit them. This course gives everyday users the recognition skills and response playbook to identify scams faster, act safely, and stop the spread.
rationale: AI has lowered the cost and raised the quality of mass scam campaigns to the point where traditional advice like 'look for spelling mistakes' is dangerously obsolete.
learning_outcome: Learners can identify AI-generated scam attempts across at least five common channels and execute a clear, confident response that protects their money and personal data.
source_signals: New Scams to Watch Out For in 2025
How To Check If A Website Is Legit Or Scam?
